name: Benin

colors.symbolism: hope and the country's lush vegetation; revival
the savannas of the north and the country's mineral wealth
the courage of the ancestors and the blood shed for independence

description: A green vertical band at the hoist taking the inner two-fifths, and at the fly two equal horizontal stripes — yellow over red — taking the outer three-fifths. The three Pan-African colours are arranged so the green forms an L-shape against the horizontal yellow-and-red bicolour.
history: Adopted on 16 November 1959 ahead of independence from France (1 August 1960) and used until 30 November 1975, when the Marxist–Leninist People's Republic of Benin replaced it with a green flag bearing a red star. The original tricolour was restored on 1 August 1990 following the abandonment of Marxism–Leninism and the transition to multiparty democracy. The colours are the classic Pan-African (Ethiopian) palette also used by Ghana, Mali, Senegal, Cameroon and others.

features.symbolism: yellow above red at the fly, two of the three Pan-African colours
the green hoist band, the third Pan-African colour, representing the hope and rebirth of the nation
